_meta.note: Full verbatim mirror of Layer 2 (all archetypes). Read-only copy, never import ml/data/** directly from app/**.

Prime-square structural shortcut
List factor sets and sum

rewrite_template.safe_generation_rule: Ask about numbers with exactly three factors or next such integer; vary target prime square.

difficulty_drivers: Question asks which sum could NOT happen, not which pair satisfies the equation
Requires integer restriction after algebraic setup
Multiple answer choices may survive a first parity filter

observable_failure_signals: Student writes xy+4=40 but never isolates xy=36
Student lists numbers that multiply to 40 rather than 36
Student gives a possible sum such as 13 or 15 when asked for NOT possible

learning_event_update_hint.positive_if: Student uses factor pairs of 36 and matches sums to choices.
learning_event_update_hint.negative_if: Student ignores integer/factor-pair constraint or answers possible instead of impossible.
learning_event_update_hint.do_not_overinfer: A wrong answer may indicate reading-direction error rather than weak algebra.

difficulty_drivers: Question asks which sum could NOT happen, not which pair satisfies the equation
Requires integer restriction after algebraic setup
Multiple answer choices may survive a first parity filter
solution_pathway_templates: Isolate xy=36, list integer factor pairs, compute each possible sum, choose the listed answer choice that never appears.
Use parity to remove impossible categories quickly, then check remaining candidate sums against factor pairs.
Set up x+y=s and xy=36 for a candidate sum; test whether integer roots/factor pairs exist.

rewrite_template.safe_generation_rule: Generate original integer product-plus-constant problems where answer choices are candidate sums; ensure exactly one listed sum is impossible under integer constraints.

difficulty_drivers: Requires combining multiple angle facts rather than applying one formula
Question asks which expression does NOT equal 180

observable_failure_signals: Student relies on visual appearance without naming angle relationships
Student cannot distinguish vertical, corresponding, and supplementary angles
Student proves a true 180 relation but selects it despite NOT prompt

learning_event_update_hint.positive_if: Student explicitly uses parallel-line and supplementary relationships to eliminate choices.
learning_event_update_hint.negative_if: Student cannot justify why each angle expression equals or does not equal 180.
learning_event_update_hint.do_not_overinfer: If the diagram was missing or unclear, treat as figure-dependency failure, not pure angle-concept failure.

difficulty_drivers: Requires combining multiple angle facts rather than applying one formula
Question asks which expression does NOT equal 180
solution_pathway_templates: Mark equal angles and supplementary pairs, then test each answer choice.
Use elimination: prove four choices equal 180 and select the remaining expression.
Trace angle measures through the parallel lines and intersection point using vertical and supplementary relationships.

rewrite_template.safe_generation_rule: Generate original parallel-line angle-chasing diagrams with expressions where all but one sum to 180.

difficulty_drivers: Students must translate “values of t that satisfy” into roots/solutions
Post-solution operation A-B is easy to miss

observable_failure_signals: Student solves for t but never assigns a,b
Student identifies roots but computes b-a
Student does not understand “satisfy equation” as solution language

learning_event_update_hint.positive_if: Student maps prompt wording to roots and performs requested comparison.
learning_event_update_hint.negative_if: Student cannot factor or misinterprets root language.
learning_event_update_hint.do_not_overinfer: If factoring is correct but A-B is wrong, update post-processing/reading more than quadratic mastery.

difficulty_drivers: Students must translate “values of t that satisfy” into roots/solutions
Post-solution operation A-B is easy to miss
solution_pathway_templates: Factor t²−6t+8=(t−2)(t−4), so roots are 2 and 4; A=4, B=2; A−B=2.
Use Vieta/product-sum: roots multiply to 8 and sum to 6, so roots are 2 and 4; difference is 2.
Use quadratic formula, then identify larger and smaller root before subtracting.

rewrite_template.safe_generation_rule: Generate original quadratic root prompts that ask for a secondary expression of the roots, not merely the roots themselves.

difficulty_drivers: Students may treat given two sides as the requested side area and stop
Requires 3D visualization and comparison of all faces

observable_failure_signals: Student answers 27 from 3×9 without finding third dimension
Student divides volume by wrong product
Student gives linear units instead of square units

learning_event_update_hint.positive_if: Student uses volume to infer missing side and compares all face areas.
learning_event_update_hint.negative_if: Student stops at the first area or fails to visualize the third dimension.
learning_event_update_hint.do_not_overinfer: A 27 answer suggests stopping/3D-representation failure more than multiplication failure.

difficulty_drivers: Students may treat given two sides as the requested side area and stop
Requires 3D visualization and comparison of all faces
solution_pathway_templates: Use V=lwh to find missing dimension 5, then compare face areas 3×9=27, 3×5=15, 9×5=45.
Draw a rectangular prism, label dimensions 3,9,5, and shade each distinct face pair.
Reason that largest face uses the two largest dimensions once all dimensions are known: 9 and 5.

rewrite_template.safe_generation_rule: Generate original rectangular prism problems where the largest face is not the initially given pair.

difficulty_drivers: Students must connect algebraic solving to graphical intersection
Arithmetic/sign errors are common under time pressure

observable_failure_signals: Student solves only one line
Student gets one coordinate correct and one wrong
Student does not know that graph intersection equals system solution

learning_event_update_hint.positive_if: Student either solves the system or verifies the correct point in both equations.
learning_event_update_hint.negative_if: Student cannot connect equations to intersection point.
learning_event_update_hint.do_not_overinfer: Arithmetic sign errors should update procedural accuracy separately from conceptual intersection meaning.

difficulty_drivers: Students must connect algebraic solving to graphical intersection
Arithmetic/sign errors are common under time pressure
solution_pathway_templates: Use elimination/substitution to solve both equations, then select the coordinate pair.
Plug each answer choice into both equations and eliminate choices quickly.
Convert to slope-intercept form only if graphing or visual reasoning is useful.

rewrite_template.safe_generation_rule: Generate original two-equation linear systems asking for the intersection point; include answer choices that expose sign and coordinate-order errors.

difficulty_drivers: Many variables and exponents increase visual load
Students may factor a non-greatest factor or mishandle zero powers/missing variables

observable_failure_signals: Student factors out a variable not present in every term
Student divides exponents by subtracting in the wrong direction
Student cannot verify by distribution

learning_event_update_hint.positive_if: Student identifies GCF and verifies by distribution.
learning_event_update_hint.negative_if: Student mishandles common variables or exponent subtraction.
learning_event_update_hint.do_not_overinfer: Dense notation may cause visual parsing errors; track representation load separately.

concept_path_template: identify common coefficient and variable powers
difficulty_drivers: Many variables and exponents increase visual load
Students may factor a non-greatest factor or mishandle zero powers/missing variables
solution_pathway_templates: Find the GCF monomial 6xy², divide each term by it, and match the resulting bracket.
Check options by distributing the factored expression back out.
Use exponent minima across terms: x¹, y², z⁰ plus coefficient GCF 6.

rewrite_template.safe_generation_rule: Generate original multivariable polynomial GCF factoring items where distractors reveal coefficient-only factoring and exponent mistakes.

difficulty_drivers: Figure is essential and raw scrape may omit image choices
Students must connect square side to circle diameter, then halve for radius

observable_failure_signals: Student gets side length 10 but fails to convert to radius 5
Student computes full circle area instead of semicircle
Student cannot identify which segment is the diameter

learning_event_update_hint.positive_if: Student moves from square area to side to diameter to radius to shaded area.
learning_event_update_hint.negative_if: Student uses the wrong circle measure or fails to read diagram.
learning_event_update_hint.do_not_overinfer: Because raw scrape lost choices/diagram, keep figure-verification flag until human confirms.

difficulty_drivers: Figure is essential and raw scrape may omit image choices
Students must connect square side to circle diameter, then halve for radius
solution_pathway_templates: Area of square 100 gives side 10; diameter 10 gives radius 5; shaded semicircle area is 1/2·π·5² = 25π/2.
Draw the square side CD as diameter and label center O, then shade half the circle and compute half of full circle area.
If choices are numeric, estimate 25π/2 ≈ 39.27 to match the closest exact/numeric option.

rewrite_template.safe_generation_rule: Generate original square-plus-semicircle shaded region problems where the square side determines the circle diameter.

archetypes.question_family: Function modeling from a threshold rate plan
archetypes.concept_path_template: Fixed base fee → included threshold → extra amount m-threshold → variable charge on extra only → simplify to equivalent linear function.
difficulty_drivers: Wordy plan hides that only additional minutes get charged; answer choices may include tempting 0.25m or 0.25m+50.
solution_pathway_templates: Set up f(m)=50+0.25(m-1000), then simplify.
Check the boundary: at m=1000, the function should charge $50.
Eliminate choices whose value at 1000 does not equal 50.
archetypes.rewrite_template: Create an original ACT-style near-transfer problem preserving the same hidden cognitive move but changing all surface numbers, context, and wording.
observable_failure_signals: student writes 0.25m or 0.25m+50 without subtracting included minutes

archetypes.question_family: Coordinate geometry line intercept from point and slope
archetypes.concept_path_template: Given slope and one point → construct y=mx+b or point-slope form → set y=0 → solve for x-intercept.
difficulty_drivers: Students may find y-intercept and stop; x-intercept requires setting y to zero.
solution_pathway_templates: Use y=mx+b, solve b, then set y=0.
Use point-slope form y-y1=m(x-x1), then set y=0 directly.
Graphically count slope from the given point down/up to the x-axis.
archetypes.rewrite_template: Create an original ACT-style near-transfer problem preserving the same hidden cognitive move but changing all surface numbers, context, and wording.
observable_failure_signals: student returns b instead of x-intercept

archetypes.concept_path_template: Read each category percent from chart → test pairs → find pairs summing to target percentage.
difficulty_drivers: Difficulty is chart extraction and pair checking, not advanced statistics.
solution_pathway_templates: List all relevant category percentages, then check pair sums.
Use answer choices as candidate pairs and add only those two chart values.
archetypes.rewrite_template: Create an original ACT-style near-transfer problem preserving the same hidden cognitive move but changing all surface numbers, context, and wording.
observable_failure_signals: student calculates pair sum correctly but uses wrong chart labels

archetypes.concept_path_template: Read category percent → convert to decimal/fraction → multiply by total number of votes.
difficulty_drivers: Students may confuse percent of total with total percent or use the wrong chart slice.
solution_pathway_templates: Compute category votes = percent × total.
Use proportion category/total = percent/100.
archetypes.rewrite_template: Create an original ACT-style near-transfer problem preserving the same hidden cognitive move but changing all surface numbers, context, and wording.
observable_failure_signals: student knows percent arithmetic but chooses wrong category

archetypes.question_family: Two-variable system from comparative money statements
archetypes.concept_path_template: Assign variables to each person → translate each comparative sentence into an equation → solve system → add quantities.
difficulty_drivers: Wording contains hypothetical changes and ratios; students often translate relation direction incorrectly.
solution_pathway_templates: Let A and K be savings; translate each sentence; substitute or eliminate; add A+K.
Backsolve from answer choices by checking whether a possible total can split into A,K satisfying both statements.
archetypes.rewrite_template: Create an original ACT-style near-transfer problem preserving the same hidden cognitive move but changing all surface numbers, context, and wording.
observable_failure_signals: student writes 2K=2A or misses one-third relationship

archetypes.concept_path_template: |linear expression| > positive number → split into expression > k OR expression < -k → solve both branches → express as outside intervals.
difficulty_drivers: Greater-than absolute value creates OR/outside intervals, counterintuitive for many students.
solution_pathway_templates: Split into two inequalities and solve.
Think distance from zero greater than 11, so outside the boundary values.
archetypes.rewrite_template: Create an original ACT-style near-transfer problem preserving the same hidden cognitive move but changing all surface numbers, context, and wording.
observable_failure_signals: student chooses middle interval answer

archetypes.concept_path_template: Two points → horizontal change and vertical change → form right triangle → distance as hypotenuse.
difficulty_drivers: Students may add coordinate differences directly or make sign errors.
solution_pathway_templates: Use distance formula.
Draw right triangle between the two coordinate points and apply Pythagorean theorem.
archetypes.rewrite_template: Create an original ACT-style near-transfer problem preserving the same hidden cognitive move but changing all surface numbers, context, and wording.
observable_failure_signals: student computes 3+6=9 or sqrt(9+36) incorrectly

archetypes.concept_path_template: log_base(input)=exponent → rewrite as base^exponent=input → solve input → if the prompt asks for a transformed value such as √input, perform that final requested operation.
difficulty_drivers: Students may over-apply log product, quotient, power, or change-of-base rules even though the problem only needs inverse-exponent translation.
Students may solve for x correctly but fail to answer the final requested expression, such as √x.
solution_pathway_templates: Convert log_5(x)=2 into 5^2=x, then answer the requested target.
Read logarithm as: what exponent on the base gives the input?
After solving for the input, re-read the prompt to check whether it asks for x, √x, or another expression.
archetypes.rewrite_template: Create an original ACT-style near-transfer problem preserving the same hidden cognitive move but changing all surface numbers, context, and wording.
observable_failure_signals: student tries product/quotient/change-of-base rules without need
student identifies x=25 but chooses wrong answer when the target is √x
student cannot distinguish base, exponent, and input

concept_path_template: inspect how the quantity changes from step to step
classify whether the task asks for a next term or a cumulative total
solution_pathway_templates: Compute successive differences or ratios to identify the pattern.
Translate repeated growth in a word problem into an arithmetic sequence.
If a total is requested, use first-term/last-term structure or average-per-term reasoning.

rewrite_template.safe_generation_rule: Generate an original question that preserves the same mathematical structure but changes the surface story, numbers, and visual presentation.

observable_failure_signals: Student stops after an intermediate computation rather than the requested target.
Student chooses an answer matching a common surface-feature trap rather than the structural relationship.

concept_path_template: identify whether the task is determinant evaluation, addition, subtraction, or scalar multiplication
apply the relevant entry-wise or determinant rule.
solution_pathway_templates: Apply the determinant formula for a 2x2 matrix.
Perform matrix addition or scalar multiplication entry by entry.
Check dimensions before combining matrices.

solution_pathway_templates: Count favorable outcomes and divide by total outcomes.
Use multiplication or permutation counting when order matters.
Check whether the question asks for a probability, a count, or a complement.

solution_pathway_templates: Factor the numbers and compare prime-power structure.
Use divisibility or modular-cycle reasoning.
Count valid integer cases carefully when the problem is combinatorial.

remediation_affordances.near_transfer: Generate a same-structure question with a small surface change.
remediation_affordances.far_transfer: Generate a different representation that still relies on the same concept.

concept_path_template: identify the relevant circle measure (radius, diameter, central angle, sector fraction, or tangent relationship)
convert that to the requested quantity.
solution_pathway_templates: Use radius-based formulas for area or circumference directly.
Convert a sector fraction into arc length or sector area.
Exploit tangency or symmetry relationships in nested-circle pictures.

combine or compare measures as needed.

Combine right-triangle geometry with a trig ratio when a side must be derived first.
